rulings.Case Title: JOANNA SEDILLO VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings On Plaintiff'S General Negligence And Products Liability Causes Of Action
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 20, 2010, line 11. Defendant City And County Of San Francisco's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings On Plaintiff's General Negligence And Products Liability Causes Of Action. Motion granted without leave to amend as to general negligence and products liability causes of action. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. The Government Code does not authorize a general negligence or products liability cause of action against a public entity and, pursuant to the California Tort Claims Act, a public entity cannot be liable to another absent an express statutory basis of liability. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: THE KAPLAN FAMILY TRUST et al VS. ZACHARY KLEINBERG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 20, 2010, line 1. DEFENDANT ZACHARY KLEINBERG'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege either that he is decedent's personal representative or that there is no personal representative and he is the successor in interest. C.C.P. section 377.30. If plaintiff alleges standing as the decedent's successor in interest, he must file an affidavit or declaration meeting the requirements set forth in C.C.P. section 377.32. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: THE KAPLAN FAMILY TRUST et al VS. ZACHARY KLEINBERG et al

rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDER TREPETIN VS. TIMOTHY MEEHAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 20, 2010, line 7. DEFENDANT TIMOTHY MEEHAN'S Motion For Attorney's Fees is denied. There is no attorney fees clause between the parties. The complaint alleges that the parties had an oral agreement. The case was voluntarily dismissed which results in no prevailing party for attorneys fees. C.C. 1717(b)(2). No finding has been made that plaintiff acted in bad faith to award attorney fees pursuant to the S.F.A.C. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ABE NEWMAN et al VS. SURESH PARMAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, UNLAWFUL DETAINER
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 20, 2010, line 10. PLAINTIFFS ABE NEWMAN, PHYLIS KURZBAD'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Triable issue of fact raised regarding whether the roof repair was a maintenance issue and defendant's responsibility, or whether the roof damage constituted a partial destruction requiring plaintiff to make the repairs and make a rent reduction. =(302/CWW)
